LAW AND MOTION 302,CROSS-DEFENDANT I2I SYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO MS. NICHOLSON'S THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE EXPRESS TERMS OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT AND THE PRESUMPTION OF LABOR CODE S2922 SHOW THAT THE CONTRACT WAS AT WILL. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IMPROPERLY SEEKS TO IMPOSE A GOOD CAUSE OR DURATION COVENANT ON AT WILL CONTRACT. PER GUZ V. BECHTEL NATIONAL, INC. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 317, 349 AND FOLEY V. INTERACTIVE DATA CORP. (1988) 47 CAL.3D 654, 698, FN.39, THE IMPLIED COVENANT CANNOT BE USED TO IMPOSE SUBSTANTIVE DUTIES OR LIMITS ON THE CONTRACTING PARTIES BEYOND THOSE INCORPORATED IN THE SPECIFIC TERMS OF THEIR AGREEMENT. THE COVENANT CANNOT BE USED TO TRANSMUTE AN AT WILL CONTRACT INTO A CONTRACT REQUIRING JUST CAUSE FOR TERMINATION. MS. NICHOLSON'S INTERPRETATION OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT AND RELATED SERIES A STOCK AGREEMENTS IS UNREASONABLE. HER EMPLOYMENT WAS AT WILL AS MATTER OF LAW. MS. NICHOLSON'S REQUEST TO STRIKE THE DEMURRER IS DENIED. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR# 6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathleen Sherman, Esq. 650-465-4940 for Plaintiff Healthcare Technology.
Eric Kastner, Esq. and J. Philip Martin, Esq. 650-967-7854 of Kastner Kim LLP for Defendant Janice Nicholson who is also present.
Martin Seeger, IV Esq. 707-526-7370 of Babin & Seeger LLP for Defendant i2i Systems.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. ALL SIDES ARGUED. The Court shares his review of the 3 motions, abuse of authority, liquidation necessary for protection, involuntary dissolution of proceedings, improper conduct of current management, suggests a local receiver and it's duties. The Court orders parties to engage in maximum discussion and exchange maximum information moving forward. Parties' stipulated request to transfer cases from Sonoma and to consolidate is granted with the lowest case number being the lead case. The Court states all its rulings, suggestions and findings on the record. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
J. Philip Martin, Esq. 650-967-7854 of Kastner Kim LLP for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Janice Nicholson.
Kathleen Sherman, Esq. 650-465-4940 for Plaintiff Healthcare and Cross-Defendants Healthcare, HTCP, Tomaino & Neece.
Martin Seeger, IV, Esq. 707-526-7370 of Babin & Seeger LLP for Defendant/Cross-Defendant i2i Systems Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides Argued. (as fuly reported on the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling in full except for the 5th and 11th causes of action. The Court rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT HEALTHCARE TECHNOLOGY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC, HTCP MANAGEMENT, LLC, MARK TOMAINO, JUSTIN NEECE AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendants Healthcare Technology Capital Partners, LLC, HTCP Management, LLC, Mark Tomaino and Justin Neece's demurrer to Ms. Nicholson's first amended cross complaint is overruled as to causes of action 1, 2, 4, 7, and 10 and sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 6, 8, and 9 for reasons set forth in the tentative ruling. (see tentative ruling for full details)

The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend by January 8, 2018 on the 5th and 11th causes of action. Ms. Nicholson is also required to enhance specific information and belief allegation. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Healthcare (Atty. Sherman) is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by January 8, 2018.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Cross-Defendants Healthcare Technology Capital Partners, Llc, Htcp Management, Llc, Mark Tomaino And Justin Neece'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross Complaint

Off calendar. Duplicate entry.

Cross-Defendant I2i Systems, Inc.'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
J. Philip Martin, Esq. 650-967-7854 of Kastner Kim LLP for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Janice Nicholson.
Kathleen Sherman, Esq. 650-465-4940 for Plaintiff Healthcare and Cross-Defendants Healthcare, HTCP, Tomaino & Neece.
Martin Seeger, IV, Esq. 707-526-7370 of Babin & Seeger LLP for Defendant/Cross-Defendant i2i Systems Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Cross-Defendant I2i Systems, Inc.'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint.

Cross-defendant i2i Systems, Inc.'s demurrer to Ms. Nicholson's third cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in the first amended cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The express terms of the employment agreement and the presumption of Labor Code s2922 show that the contract was at will. The third cause of action improperly seeks to impose a good cause or duration covenant on at will contract. Per Guz v. Bechtel National, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 317, 349 and Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 698, fn.39, the implied covenant cannot be used to impose substantive duties or limits on the contracting parties beyond those incorporated in the specific terms of their agreement. The covenant cannot be used to transmute an at will contract into a contract requiring just cause for termination. Ms. Nicholson's interpretation of the employment agreement and related Series A stock agreements is unreasonable. Her employment was at will as matter of law. Ms. Nicholson's request to strike the demurrer is denied. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: J. Phillip Martin and Eric C. Kastner, counsel for Defendant Janice Nicholson and specially appearing for Defendant Kenneth Dansie; Martin L. Seeger, IV, counsel for cross-defendant I2I Systems, Inc.; Kathleen Sherman, counsel for Plaintiff, Healthcare Technology Capital Partners LLC and HTCP Management LLC.

The Court presents a written tentative ruling to counsel denying the motion for appointment of receiver noting the want of diminution in value of the company. Prior to addressing the tentative ruling, consel note various upcoming hearing dates whereupon the Court notes that since this is not a single assignment case, those hearing dates are not before him at this time.

Mr. Martin and Mr. Seeger argue the motion with Mr. Martin noting various limited financial information with respect to quarterly losses to support the diminution of value argument. Mr. Seeger contends that there is, as the Court notes, insufficient evidence to support the diminution of value argument, noting that the defenants failed to take the deposition of the current CFO

After futher argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling and denies the motion for appointment of receiver.

The Court notes that this case appears to be ripe for settlement and advises counsel that it has approached Judge Mary Wiss for her services in that regard. After consultation with their respective clients,counsel advise the Court that they are amiable to settlement efforts and recess to Judge Wiss' chambers for further discussion.

The Motions to Seal Confidential Documents are not addressed at this time.
